params.name: Bronze Wine Vessel
params.description:
On Apr 4, 1978 AD, the Bronze Wine Vessel was excavated from the Zenghouyi Tomb in Hubei.
This bronze vessel dates back to the early Warring States period and is the most complex and exquisite of all the bronze artifacts excavated from that period. It consists of a vessel and a plate, both with intricate hollowed-out patterns, over a dozen intertwined dragon and chimaera decorations, and an inscription displaying its owner's identity. Due to its complex patterns and the lack of forging marks, …
